quote: PDD Holdings FY2024 Revenue: $54.0B, Net Income: $15.4B, Operating Income: $14.9B. Revenue +55% YoY, Net Income +82% YoY.
Transaction Services revenue doubled from RMB 94.1B (FY2023) to RMB 195.9B (FY2024), +108% YoY. Online Marketing Services: RMB 197.9B, +29% YoY.
Q1 2025 Revenue: RMB 95.67B (~$13.18B), +10% YoY. Operating Profit: RMB 16.09B ($2.22B), -38% YoY. Net Income: RMB 14.74B ($2.03B), -47% YoY.
Q2 2025 Revenue: RMB 103.98B ($14.5B), +7% YoY. Non-GAAP Operating Margin: 27% (down from 36% in Q2 2024). Non-GAAP EPS beat consensus of $2.16 by 43%.
Q3 2025 Revenue: RMB 108.28B ($15.2B), +9% YoY. Net Income: RMB 29.33B ($4.12B), +17% YoY. Cash: RMB 423.8B ($59.5B). Operating cash flow: RMB 45.7B ($6.4B), up from RMB 27.5B YoY.

CEO Chen Lei: 'Upgraded from RMB 10B fee reduction program to RMB 100B comprehensive support program.' VP Finance Jun Liu: 'Prioritizing long-term intrinsic value over short-term financial performance.'

Q3 2025 call: 'Current quarter profitability should not be used as indicator for future quarters.' Regulatory changes 'remain significant, bringing greater challenges and uncertainty.' AI was NOT mentioned at all.
Chairman Chen Lei stated company is 'in an investment phase' and faces 'significant competition and external uncertainties' — buybacks/dividends 'not anticipated in the foreseeable future.'
PDD stock price ~$103.84. 52-week high: $139.41 (Oct 29, 2025). 52-week low: $87.11 (Apr 10, 2025). P/E: 10.03. Market cap: ~$148B. 5-year return: -33.31%.
Analyst consensus: Moderate Buy. 7 Buy / 4 Hold / 0 Sell. Average price target: $146.39. Morningstar fair value: $297.00.
Temu was the #1 most downloaded shopping app worldwide in 2024 across 15 major markets including US, UK, Germany, France, Japan, Brazil.
Temu had 363 million downloads globally in 2025, maintaining #1 in shopping category.
Cumulative global downloads exceeded 1.2 billion as of October 2025. Global MAU reached 530M in August 2025 (record high).
Q2 2025: EU MAU 141.6M (+74% YoY), 34% of global user base. LatAm MAU +122% YoY. US MAU dropped 28% YoY, reduced to 11% of global users.
After de minimis elimination, Temu cut US ad impressions by 97%. US install base dropped to 68M users in June 2025, recovered to 91M by October 2025.
After May 2025 de minimis ban on China: 85% drop in low-value parcel volume — from ~4M/day to ~600K/day. Aug 29, 2025: Global de minimis exemption ended for ALL countries.
Chinese 3PL operators and e-commerce companies accounted for 20% of net leasing of new US warehouse space (Prologis, Q3 2025). Temu announced it won't change prices for US customers despite tariffs.
By Oct 2025: 13 self-owned warehouses globally (10 Europe, 2 US, 1 near US-Mexico border). 14th planned in Rio de Janeiro. Delivery times improved to 2-5 days for items from US warehouses.
European warehouses projected to handle 80% of European orders domestically by 2026. Temu Local Seller Program expanded to 30+ markets by Oct 2025.
Temu Trustpilot rating: 2.5/5 stars from 48,741 reviews. Sitejabber: 2/5 from 1,760 reviews. ComplaintsBoard: 0% resolution rate on 150 complaints.
Rate of likely AI-generated reviews on Trustpilot increased 1,361% from 2022-2025. 2022: 0.75% AI reviews; 2025: 10.90%.
EU Commission preliminarily found Temu in breach of DSA. Mystery shopping found consumers 'very likely to find non-compliant products.' Potential fine: up to 6% of global turnover.
Dec 10, 2025: EU Commission raided Temu's Dublin HQ under Foreign Subsidies Regulation. Second-ever FSR dawn raid. Potential penalty: up to 10% of global annual turnover.
EU Council voted to eliminate EUR 150 de minimis. Jul 1, 2026: EUR 3 flat-rate duty per consignment. 2028: full normal tariffs. In 2024, ~4.6B parcels entered EU under de minimis, >90% from China.
Phase 1 (Aug 29, 2025 - Feb 28, 2026): Flat per-item duty $80-$200. Phase 2 (Mar 1, 2026): Full ad valorem tariffs based on country-of-origin schedule.
Temu lost 58% of US daily active users post-tariff. Cargo from China reduced by up to 60%.
Pinduoduo app recorded 540.57 million MAU in China, August 2025 (iiMedia Research).
QuestMobile: Pinduoduo is the only major e-commerce app with relatively significant MAU growth since 2022; DAU/MAU ratio has gradually surpassed Taobao.
China e-commerce market share 2024: Alibaba 41-44% (down from 52% in 2021), JD 24%, Pinduoduo 19% and rising (up from 7.2% in 2019).
Douyin e-commerce: GMV growth exceeded 30% in first 10 months of 2025. Shelf e-commerce GMV grew 49% YoY. Merchant subsidies >RMB 19B disbursed by Aug 2025.
Douyin 2024 GMV: ~3.5T yuan (+30% YoY). 2025 target: surpass 4T yuan, approaching Pinduoduo. Shelf e-commerce share of Douyin GMV: 30% (2023), 40% (2024), ~50% (2025).
Agricultural product sales on Pinduoduo increased 47% YoY in H1 2025. Post-2000 (Gen Z) agricultural merchants grew >30%.
Dec 2025: Co-Chairman Zhao Jiazhen announced plan to 'recreate a Pinduoduo' by 'heavily investing in China's supply chain' over next 3 years, leveraging demand pool covering 90+ countries.
China manufacturing capacity utilization 2025: 74.6%. Q2 2025: 74.0% (lowest since Q1 2020). Historical average: 75.75%.
SAMR raided PDD facilities in Shanghai under suspicion of fraudulent dealings and illegal subsidies, same week as Temu Dublin HQ raid.
PDD fired dozens of workers after a 'fistfight with China officials' during SAMR inspection.
SAMR 2026 priorities: step up antitrust enforcement, rein in 'cutthroat competition,' better develop platform economy.
Cybersecurity Law revision effective January 1, 2026. Includes AI governance provisions, increased penalties up to RMB 10 million, broader extraterritorial reach.
China 2025 retail sales: +3.7% YoY, totaling 50.12 trillion yuan. Online retail: +8.6% to 15.97 trillion yuan. Physical goods online: 26.1% of total retail.
World Bank GDP projections: China 4.5% (2025), 4.0% (2026).
Alibaba FY2026 Q3: Revenue RMB 284.8B ($41.4B), missed estimate of RMB 290.7B. Cloud +36%, AI-related revenue triple-digit growth for 10th consecutive quarter.
JD.com FY2025: Revenue RMB 1,309.1B ($187.2B), +13%. Active customers >700M. JD Logistics 130+ overseas warehouses, 23 countries.
Shein 2024 revenue: $38B (+18%). Q1 2025 revenue surged to ~$10B as consumers front-loaded purchases. Valuation collapsed from ~$100B (2022) to ~$10B (Aug 2025).
Amazon Haul: only 16% of Americans use monthly vs 28% Temu, 23% Shein. All products under $20, 1-2 week delivery.
TikTok Shop 2024 global GMV: $33.2B. 2025E: ~$66B. 2026E: ~$112B. US buyers 2025: 53.2M. US shops grew from ~4,450 (mid-2023) to ~475,000 (mid-2025).
China precautionary savings: net new household savings deposits in H1 2025: 17.94 trillion RMB (up from 11.46 trillion in H1 2024).
Hakuhodo 2024 survey: 80% of Chinese consumers opted for cheaper products. Luxury consumption contracted 20% in 2024 (Bain). CPI below 1% for 28 consecutive months.
US CBP processed ~4.5M Section 321 informal entries per day in 2024. Section 321 entries: 95.1% of all US import entries. De minimis entries grew from 134M (2015) to 1.36B annually (2024).
Temu's US operator agreed to $2M federal penalty for INFORM Consumers Act transparency failures. Oklahoma and Nebraska opened reviews into deceptive practices.
temu.com: 2.11B visits in June 2025, avg session 9m02s. US accounted for 31.6% (~665M visits). Users spend avg 21 min/day vs Amazon 10 min.
China e-commerce GMV share: Alibaba ~32%, PDD ~23%, Douyin ~18-20% (approaching PDD), JD ~15-17%.
End of 2025: 1.125 billion internet users in China, penetration rate 80.1%.
Temu and Shein each shipped ~900,000 packages daily in US (July 2024, ShipMatrix).
PDD 20-F: VIE structure risk — cash held by VIE/China subsidiaries may not be available outside mainland China. Pinduoduo app data access vulnerabilities. Class action filed Aug 2024.
PDD Q4 2025 / FY2025 full year results scheduled for March 25, 2026 before US market open. Consensus EPS estimate: $3.04.
~10M TEU of container ship capacity on order (1/3 of current active fleet). SCFI Shanghai-US West Coast: $1,460/FEU — below carrier break-even. 2026 US ocean imports projected to contract 2%.
Despite tariffs, US bargain shoppers returning: site visits, app installs, shopping frequency surged to match 2024 levels. 29% report higher Temu prices, 24% higher Shein prices.
PDD 100-billion-yuan merchant support initiative (2025): includes fee reductions, subsidies for SME sellers, cross-border support.
State Council Decree No. 810: all internet platforms must report tax-related info to Chinese tax authorities (effective Oct 1, 2025). >4,100 platforms including Pinduoduo submitted first round.
86% of US adults aware of Temu in 2025. 4 in 10 US adults made a purchase on Temu. Users spend avg 21 min/day in mobile app vs Amazon 10 min.
